query: Draft a suit for partition and separate possession of ancestral joint family property situated at Bangalore. The Plaintiff and Defendants are Hindu co-owners who inherited the property from their deceased father. The property includes a residential house at No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore measuring 2400 sq.ft. and agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural measuring 2 acres. The Defendants are denying the Plaintiff's rightful 1/3rd share and refusing to partition. Claim mesne profits for exclusion from possession. Include genealogy table, schedule of properties, and prayer for appointment of Commissioner. Draft suitable for filing before the City Civil Court, Bangalore.

v4.draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT, BANGALORE

Partition suit for partition and separate possession of ancestral joint family property situated at Bangalore, comprising a residential house at No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore measuring 2400 sq.ft. and agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural measuring 2 acres; seeking 1/3rd share, partition by metes and bounds, appointment of Commissioner to effect partition, and mesne profits for exclusion from possession.

Plaintiff: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}. ... Plaintiff

Vs.

Defendant: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}. ... Defendant

JURISDICTION

The Plaintiff and Defendants are Hindu co-owners of ancestral joint family property. The suit property is situated within the territorial jurisdiction of the City Civil Court, Bangalore. The cause of action for partition arises at Bangalore in respect of immovable properties, and the valuation falls within the pecuniary limits of the City Civil Court. The relief sought is partition and separate possession with appointment of a Commissioner under Order XX Rule 18 CPC, and mesne profits for exclusion from possession. All requisite causes of action arise within the territorial limits of Bangalore, and thus this Honle Court has jurisdiction to entertain and try this suit.

FACTS OF THE CASE

1. The Plaintiff and the Defendants are Hindu co-owners of an ancestral joint family property, having inherited the same from their deceased father, on whose death the Hindu succession rules of coparcenary applied.

2. The property comprises two schedules: (a) Residential house No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore measuring 2400 sq.ft.; and (b) Agricultural land, Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural measuring 2 acres.

3. The property is held as ancestral joint family property, with rights of coparcenary in the male line, and the Plaintiff claims a 1/3rd share as a co-owner.

4. The Plaintiff has repeatedly requested the Defendants to partition the joint family property and to grant the Plaintiff his rightful share, but the Defendants have denied the Plaintiffs entitlement and refused to effect any partition.

5. The Plaintiff has remained in possession of his share only as a co-owner and continues to be excluded from exclusive possession of any specific portion until partition is effected.

6. To enforce the Plaintiffs rights and to avoid further dispute, the Plaintiff seeks partition by metes and bounds and separate possession of his 1/3rd share, along with appointment of a Commissioner to make the partition.

7. The Plaintiff also seeks mesne profits for the period during which the Plaintiff has been excluded from possession due to the Defendants refusal to partition and hand over possession.

8. Genealogy of the family and the joint nature of the property are annexed herewith as Annexure A (Genealogy table).

9. Schedule of properties detailing the properties, their location and extent is annexed herewith as Annexure B (Schedule of properties).

10. Copies of title deeds/ownership documents and succession proof are annexed herewith as Annexure C and Annexure D.

11. Any prior communications and demands made by the Plaintiff in respect of partition are annexed herewith as Annexure E.

LEGAL BASIS

12. Order XX Rule 18 CPC enables the Court to order partition of immovable property held in common and to appoint a Commissioner to make such partition and report back to the Court. The present suit is for partition and separate possession; hence Order XX Rule 18 CPC provides a direct procedural basis for appointment of a Commissioner to effect the partition.

13. Hindu Succession Act, 1956: Sections 6, 8 and 10 recognise coparcenary rights and devolution of interest in joint family property, thereby underpinning the Plaintiffs entitlement to a share and the right to seek partition and separate possession.

14. Partition Act, 1893: Sections 2, 3 and 4 empower Courts to determine and declare rights in joint family properties and to effect partitions by metes and bounds, including appointment of Commissioners where necessary.

15. Indian Succession Act, 1925: Sections 211 and 218 relate to succession and the distribution of estate of a deceased person, supporting the acquisition of rights by the Plaintiff as a coparcener and heir and the entitlement to pursue partition and possession.

16. The Defendantsefensive posture that there is no prior partition is anticipated; however, no partition has taken place, and the Plaintiff remains a co-owner with a right to enforce partition and possession under the cited statutes.

17. The Plaintiff seeks appointment of a Commissioner under Order XX Rule 18 CPC to demarcate and deliver the Plaintiffs 1/3rd share by metes and bounds and to report to the Court for final decree.

CAUSE OF ACTION

Origin and accrual: The Plaintiff's cause of action arises on the death of the father, when coparcenary rights in the ancestral joint family property attached to the Plaintiff as a Hindu coparcener and co-owner. The Defendants' ongoing denial of partition and possession constitutes a continuing cause of action, and the Plaintiff's rights remain unsettled so long as partition remains undetermined.

LIMITATION
No separate limitation for partition; the action is filed within a reasonable time from knowledge of exclusion.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

The value of plaintiff's share and the partition claim shall be assessed for ad valorem court fee purposes. The Plaintiff's share is 1/3rd of the scheduled properties, and the Court fee payable shall be computed as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act on the basis of the value of relief sought.

PRAYER
The Plaintiff respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(a) Pass a preliminary decree partitioning the ancestral joint family properties and grant separate possession of the Plaintiff's 1/3rd share by metes and bounds, to be drawn by a Commissioner; the value of Plaintiff's share being Rs. {{SHARE_VALUE}}.
(b) Appoint a Commissioner to make the partition of the immovable properties described in Schedule of Properties (Annexure B) and report its findings to this Court for final decree.
(c) Award mesne profits to the Plaintiff for the period of exclusion from possession at Rs. {{MESNE_PROFITS_RATE}} per month (or rate) from the date of exclusion until delivery; subject to Court's final determination.
(d) Costs of this suit; and such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

19. Annexure A  Genealogy table (family lineage and coparcener status)

20. Annexure B  Schedule of properties (No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore; Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural; extent 2400 sq.ft. and 2 acres respectively)

21. Annexure C  Copy of death certificate of the father and succession documents (demonstrating inheritance)

22. Annexure D  Copies of title deeds / ownership documents for Schedule properties

23. Annexure E  Correspondence/demands/communications relating to partition

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby solemnly verify and state on oath that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 11 of the plaint are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief, and that the facts relating to the genealogy and the schedule of properties annexed as Annexure A and Annexure B are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief. I further state that no material fact has been concealed and that this verification is made in the City Civil Court, Bangalore, on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}

v5.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT, BANGALORE

AT BANGALORE

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR PARTITION AND SEPARATE POSSESSION OF ANCESTRAL JOINT FAMILY PROPERTY AND MESNE PROFITS

UNDER ORDER XX, RULE 18 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908
READ WITH SECTIONS 6, 8, 10 OF THE HINDU SUCCESSION ACT, 1956; SECTIONS 2-4 OF THE PARTITION ACT, 1893

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a Hindu coparcener and co-owner of the joint family property; The Defendants are also co-owners of the same ancestral property.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit since the properties situate at No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore and Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural fall within the jurisdiction of this Court, and the cause of action accrued within the local limits of this Court.

FACTS OF THE CASE

3. The Plaintiff and Defendants inherited the aforesaid properties from their deceased father, Shri {{FATHER_NAME}}, who died on {{FATHER_DEATH_DATE}}; The properties are ancestral in nature and remain coparcenary joint family property of the Plaintiff and Defendants.

4. The properties comprise (i) a residential house at No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore measuring 2400 sq.ft.; and (ii) an agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural measuring 2 acres. The Plaintiff claims a 1/3rd share in the aforesaid properties and seeks partition and separate possession of his share.

5. The Defendants deny the Plaintiff’s rightful 1/3rd share and refuse to partition the joint family property, thereby depriving the Plaintiff of his legal entitlement.

6. The Plaintiff has been excluded from possession of the aforesaid properties by the Defendants since {{EXCLUSION_DATE}} and has been deprived of his rightful share and enjoyment.

7. The Plaintiff is ready and willing to partition the joint family property by metes and bounds and to give effect to his 1/3rd share, and to obtain separate possession.

8. It is necessary and appropriate that a Commissioner be appointed to effect partition of the aforesaid properties and to file a report before this Hon'ble Court.

CAUSE OF ACTION

9. The cause of action for partition first accrued on the death of Shri {{FATHER_NAME}} and the subsequent denial of partition by the Defendants; The cause of action is continuing as the Defendants persist in denying partition and exclusion continues.

10. The Plaintiff asserts a claim for mesne profits for exclusion from possession since {{EXCLUSION_DATE}} until the date of decree, the amount to be determined by the Court and in accordance with law.

LEGAL BASIS

11. The suit is maintainable under Order XX, Rule 18 CPC for partition and separate possession of the joint ancestral property.

12. In the alternative, the Plaintiff relies on the following statutory provisions:

    (i) Hindu Succession Act, 1956: Section 6, Section 8, Section 10;  
    (ii) Partition Act, 1893: Section 2, Section 3, Section 4;  
    (iii) Indian Succession Act, 1925: Section 211, Section 218.

LIMITATION

13. There is no applicable limitation prescribed for partition; this is a continuing cause of action. If ouster/exclusion is pleaded, the accrual begins from the date of exclusion. No Limitation Act article is cited in this plaint.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

14. The suit is valued for purposes of jurisdiction and court fee at Rs. {{SUIT_VALUE}} (Rupees {{SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only). The requisite court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE}} has been paid.

INTEREST

15. The Plaintiff claims mesne profits for exclusion from possession, which may be determined by the Court and the Commissioner, and shall be payable from the date of exclusion until realisation, as per applicable law.

PRAYER

16. In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

    (a) Declare that the Plaintiff is entitled to 1/3rd share in the joint ancestral properties described above and to partition the same by metes and bounds;

    (b) Pass a decree for partition and separate possession in terms of the partition so as to grant the Plaintiff his 1/3rd share;

    (c) Appoint a Commissioner under Section 2 of the Partition Act, 1893, and under Order XX, Rule 18 CPC, to effect the partition and submit the report to this Court with a plan of partition and separate possession;

    (d) Pass such preliminary/final decree as may be necessary to give effect to the partition and separate possession;

    (e) Award mesne profits for exclusion from possession from the date of exclusion {{EXCLUSION_DATE}} to the date of decree, and for such future period as the Court may deem fit;

    (f) Award costs of the suit including court fees, advocate’s fees, and any other pre-litigation costs incurred; and

    (g) Grant such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

AND FOR THIS ACT OF KINDNESS, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL, AS IN DUTY BOUND, EVER PRAY.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A: Genealogy Table (to be filed with plaint)

Annexure B: Schedule of Properties (Residential house at No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore; Agricultural land at Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural)

Annexure C: Copy of title deeds and death certificate of Shri {{FATHER_NAME}} and other related ownership documents

Annexure D: Any other relevant documents in support of partition and entitlement

GENEALOGY TABLE (Annexure A)

[This table is to be filled with actual names by the parties. A sample skeleton is provided for guidance.]

Shri {{FATHER_NAME}} (deceased)
├── {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}} (son, aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years)
└── {{DEFENDANT_NAME}} (son, aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years)

SCHEDULE OF PROPERTIES (Annexure B)

17. Residential House: No. 42, 3rd Cross, Jayanagar, Bangalore
   - Extent: 2400 sq.ft.
   - Nature: Ancestral joint family property; co-owned by Plaintiff and Defendants

18. Agricultural Land: Survey No. 85, Anekal Taluk, Bangalore Rural
   - Extent: 2 acres
   - Nature: Ancestral joint family property; co-owned by Plaintiff and Defendants

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify and state on oath that the contents of paras 1 to 16 of this plaint are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief, that the facts stated herein are based on records and documents in my possession, and that no part of the cause of action has arisen in any other jurisdiction. Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

                                                        {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

                                                        PLAINTIFF

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}


ADVOCATE FOR THE PLAINTIFF

Enrollment No.: {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}

Address: {{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
